$comment: Snorkel's approved canonical base images, verbatim from documentation/snorkel_canonical_image_best_practices.md. The submission form asks 'Does this task use an approved canonical base image?' and that is an ATTESTATION — so it is computed against this list, never assumed. See apps/worker/src/stages/canonical-image.ts.
$conflict: THE DOCS DISAGREE WITH EACH OTHER, and the disagreement is two characters long. 'Dockerfile & Image Best Practices.txt' gives golang@sha256:...89540ce6173ea77ac, while snorkel_canonical_image_best_practices.md — the file the playbook itself designates as canonical — gives ...89540c06173ea77ac. Our own prompts/summary.txt contained BOTH, so a Go task could be built against either, and one of them is not on the list. This file follows the canonical doc. If a Go task ever fails Snorkel's check on the base image, this is the first place to look.
